Midterm Project- R Code and Outputs

Load in neccessary packages

library(tidymodels)
library(caret)
library(corrplot)
library(glmnet)
library(kableExtra)
library(earth)
library(dplyr)

Part I

Using this dataset, please help Researcher A build a prediction model for PFS, aiming to understand how demographic, tumor, and treatment characteristics influence progression-free survival in breast cancer patients.

To answer this question, your report should at least include the following:

Model Training: Provide a detailed description of the model training procedure and how you obtained the model. Your description should include sufficient detail so that another statistician can reproduce the same model. Points will be deducted if the details provided are insufficient to reproduce your results.

Import and partition data using ‘tidymodels’ package

load("data/datA.RData")
datA <- datA |>
  mutate(
    menopause = as.factor(menopause),
    race = as.factor(race),
    tumor_grade = as.factor(tumor_grade),
    ER = as.factor(ER),
    PR = as.factor(PR),
    HER2 = as.factor(HER2),
    stage = as.factor(stage),
    surgery = as.factor(surgery),
    chemo = as.factor(chemo),
    hormonal_therapy = as.factor(hormonal_therapy)
  )

set.seed(2)

datA_split = initial_split(datA, prop = 0.8)

# Extract the training and test data
training_datA = training(datA_split)
testing_datA = testing(datA_split)

# Training data
x_trainA = model.matrix(log_PFS ~ . - id, training_datA)[, -1]
y_trainA = training_datA$log_PFS

# Test data
x_testA = model.matrix(log_PFS ~ . - id, testing_datA)[, -1]
y_testA = testing_datA$log_PFS

# Remove ID
training_datA <- training_datA |>  select(-"id")
testing_datA  <- testing_datA  |> select(-"id")

Exploratory Data Analysis

# Changing the plot display
theme1 <- trellis.par.get()
theme1$plot.symbol$col <- rgb(.2, .4, .2, .5)
theme1$plot.symbol$pch <- 16
theme1$plot.line$col <- rgb(.8, .1, .1, 1)
theme1$plot.line$lwd <- 2
theme1$strip.background$col <- rgb(.0, .2, .6, .2)
trellis.par.set(theme1)

# Feature plots to look at trends between predictors and the outcome (log_PFS)
featurePlot(x = training_datA[ ,c(1, 4, 5, 11)],
            y = training_datA[ ,16],
            plot = "scatter",
            span = .5,
            labels = c("Predictors","log_PFS"),
            type = c("p", "smooth"),
            layout = c(2, 2))

# Calculates the correlation matrix for all the predictors
corrplot(cor(x_trainA), method = "circle", type = "full")

This data set includes 7 binary predictors, 3 categorical predictors, 5 continuous predictors, and 1 continuous outcome in log scale.

Chose elastic net because continuous variables formed a cloud, meaning we could assume linearity? homoscedasticity?

Correlation plot revealed that certain variables were highly correlated. Chose to address this with elastic net because we can address colinearity for [] and [] reasons

# Elastic Net

set.seed(2)
ctrl1 <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10) # set up cv
set.seed(2) # set seed for reproduction

enet.fit <- train(log_PFS ~ ., 
                  data = training_datA, 
                  method = "glmnet",
                  tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = seq(0, 1, length = 21),
                                         lambda = exp(seq(-1, -7, length = 100))),
                  trControl = ctrl1)
## Warning in nominalTrainWorkflow(x = x, y = y, wts = weights, info = trainInfo,
## : There were missing values in resampled performance measures.
enet.fit$bestTune
##      alpha     lambda
## 2047     1 0.01481491
myCol <- rainbow(25)
myPar <- list(superpose.symbol = list(col = myCol),
superpose.line = list(col = myCol))

plot(enet.fit, par.settings = myPar, xTrans = log)

# Coefficients in the final model
coef(enet.fit$finalModel, enet.fit$bestTune$lambda)
## 20 x 1 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix"
##                    s=0.01481491
## (Intercept)        2.6596549020
## age               -0.0025719864
## menopause1         .           
## race2              .           
## race3              0.0108623481
## race4             -0.0244504922
## bmi                .           
## tumor_size        -0.0545380491
## tumor_grade2       .           
## tumor_grade3      -0.1057179919
## lymph_nodes       -0.0540172355
## ER1                0.1709296973
## PR1                .           
## HER21             -0.1732033529
## Ki67              -0.0046400332
## stage2             .           
## stage3             .           
## surgery1          -0.0009660964
## chemo1             .           
## hormonal_therapy1  0.1589522549

Results: Report the model. Interpret the model and assess its performance.

# Predict on testing_datA
enet.pred <- predict(enet.fit, newdata = testing_datA)

rmse_A <- RMSE(enet.pred, testing_datA$log_PFS)
r2_A  <- R2(enet.pred,   testing_datA$log_PFS)
# Create table with the optimal tuning parameters for the chosen model
opt_para <- enet.fit$bestTune
rownames(opt_para) <- NULL
opt_para |> 
  kable()
alpha lambda
1 0.0148149 
# Create a table with the model coefficients
coefs1 <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(coef(enet.fit$finalModel, enet.fit$bestTune$lambda)))
coefs1$Predictors <- rownames(coefs1)
colnames(coefs1)[1] <- "Coefficients"
coefs1 <- coefs1[, c("Predictors", "Coefficients")]
rownames(coefs1) <- NULL
coefs1 |>
  kable()
Predictors Coefficients
(Intercept) 2.6596549
age -0.0025720
menopause1 0.0000000
race2 0.0000000
race3 0.0108623
race4 -0.0244505
bmi 0.0000000
tumor_size -0.0545380
tumor_grade2 0.0000000
tumor_grade3 -0.1057180
lymph_nodes -0.0540172
ER1 0.1709297
PR1 0.0000000
HER21 -0.1732034
Ki67 -0.0046400
stage2 0.0000000
stage3 0.0000000
surgery1 -0.0009661
chemo1 0.0000000
hormonal_therapy1 0.1589523 
# Create a table with the exponentiated coefficients
coefs2 <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(coef(enet.fit$finalModel, enet.fit$bestTune$lambda)))
coefs2$Predictors <- rownames(coefs2)
colnames(coefs2)[1] <- "Exponentiated Coefficients"
coefs2 <- coefs2[, c("Predictors", "Exponentiated Coefficients")]
coefs2 <- coefs2 |>
  filter(Predictors %in% c("(Intercept)", "age", "tumor_size", "tumor_grade", "lymph_nodes", 
                           "ER", "HER2", "Ki67", "hormonal_therapy"))
coefs2$`Exponentiated Coefficients`<- exp(coefs2$`Exponentiated Coefficients`)
rownames(coefs2) <- NULL
coefs2 |>
  kable()
Predictors Exponentiated Coefficients
(Intercept) 14.2913563
age 0.9974313
tumor_size 0.9469225
lymph_nodes 0.9474158
Ki67 0.9953707 
# Create table with model performance results
tibble(
  "RMSE" = rmse_A,
  "R^2" = r2_A
) |> 
  kable()
RMSE R^2
0.7465867 0.1685384

Part II

About a decade later, Researcher B conducted a separate study enrolling 2000 breast cancer patients diagnosed between 2000 and 2005, using the same study design and variable definitions. Like Researcher A’s study, all patients were followed until disease progression or death. Researcher B’s data are stored in datB.RData. Researcher A would like to evaluate whether the prediction model built on the original data generalizes well to Researcher B’s dataset. Does the model generalize well? If yes, provide justification. If not, build a new prediction model for the 2000–2005 cohort and discuss what has changed in prediction.

Import dataset and create matrix

load("data/datB.RData")

datB <- datB |>
  mutate(
    menopause = as.factor(menopause),
    race = as.factor(race),
    tumor_grade = as.factor(tumor_grade),
    ER = as.factor(ER),
    PR = as.factor(PR),
    HER2 = as.factor(HER2),
    stage = as.factor(stage),
    surgery = as.factor(surgery),
    chemo = as.factor(chemo),
    hormonal_therapy = as.factor(hormonal_therapy)
  )

# Remove id for matrix operations
all_datB <- datB |> select(-"id")

Evaluate the model’s performance on researcher B’s dataset

# Use all of datB to test generalizability of elastic net model
enet_pred_B <- predict(enet.fit, newdata = all_datB)

# Compare performances
rmse_B <- RMSE(enet_pred_B, all_datB$log_PFS)

# Report table comparing performance
performance_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("datA (Internal Test Set)", "datB (External Validation)"),
  RMSE = c(round(rmse_A, 4), round(rmse_B, 4))
)

colnames(performance_table) <- c("Dataset", "RMSE")

performance_table |>
  kable(caption = "Elastic Net Model Performance: Internal vs External Validation") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
Elastic Net Model Performance: Internal vs External Validation
Dataset RMSE
datA (Internal Test Set) 0.7466
datB (External Validation) 0.8363

Creation of New Model

Import and partition data using ‘tidymodels’ package

set.seed(2)

datB_split = initial_split(datB, prop = 0.8)

# Extract the training and test data
training_datB = training(datB_split)
testing_datB = testing(datB_split)

# Training data
x_trainB = model.matrix(log_PFS ~ . - id, training_datB)[, -1]
y_trainB = training_datB$log_PFS

# Test data
x_testB  = model.matrix(log_PFS ~ . - id, testing_datB)[, -1]
y_testB = testing_datB$log_PFS

# Remove ID
training_datB <- training_datB |>  select(-"id")
testing_datB  <- testing_datB  |> select(-"id")

Exploratory Data Analysis

# Feature plots to look at trends between predictors and the outcome (log_PFS)
featurePlot(x = training_datB[ ,c(1, 4, 5, 11)],
            y = training_datB[ ,16],
            plot = "scatter",
            span = .5,
            labels = c("Predictors","log_PFS"),
            type = c("p", "smooth"),
            layout = c(2, 2))

# Calculates the correlation matrix for all the predictors
corrplot(cor(x_trainB), method = "circle", type = "full")

Elastic Net model

# Elastic Net

set.seed(2)
ctrl1 <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10) # set up cv
set.seed(2) # set seed for reproduction

enet.test.b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., 
                  data = training_datB, 
                  method = "glmnet",
                  tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = seq(0, 1, length = 21),
                                         lambda = exp(seq(-1, -7, length = 100))),
                  trControl = ctrl1)
## Warning in nominalTrainWorkflow(x = x, y = y, wts = weights, info = trainInfo,
## : There were missing values in resampled performance measures.
enet.test.b$bestTune
##    alpha    lambda
## 91     0 0.2132149
myCol <- rainbow(25)
myPar <- list(superpose.symbol = list(col = myCol),
superpose.line = list(col = myCol))

plot(enet.test.b, par.settings = myPar, xTrans = log)

# Coefficients in the final model
coef(enet.test.b$finalModel, enet.test.b$bestTune$lambda)
## 20 x 1 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix"
##                    s=0.2132149
## (Intercept)        2.998651952
## age               -0.002471616
## menopause1        -0.008133823
## race2             -0.040104815
## race3             -0.024429393
## race4              0.068951230
## bmi               -0.001545360
## tumor_size        -0.042193986
## tumor_grade2       0.074766605
## tumor_grade3      -0.106566952
## lymph_nodes       -0.016611495
## ER1                0.144906908
## PR1                0.096289175
## HER21              0.024744777
## Ki67              -0.004773993
## stage2             0.003120753
## stage3            -0.074148475
## surgery1          -0.025326354
## chemo1            -0.030902468
## hormonal_therapy1  0.165089624
# Predict on testing_datB
enet.pred.b <- predict(enet.test.b, newdata = testing_datB)

rmse_testB <- RMSE(enet.pred.b, testing_datB$log_PFS)
r2_testB  <- R2(enet.pred.b, testing_datB$log_PFS)
# Create table with the optimal tuning parameters for the chosen model
opt_para <- enet.test.b$bestTune
rownames(opt_para) <- NULL
opt_para |> 
  kable()
alpha lambda
0 0.2132149 
# Create a table with the model coefficients
coefs1 <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(coef(enet.test.b$finalModel, enet.test.b$bestTune$lambda)))
coefs1$Predictors <- rownames(coefs1)
colnames(coefs1)[1] <- "Coefficients"
coefs1 <- coefs1[, c("Predictors", "Coefficients")]
rownames(coefs1) <- NULL
coefs1 |>
  kable()
Predictors Coefficients

coefs2 <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(coef(enet.test.b$finalModel, enet.test.b$bestTune$lambda)))
coefs2$Predictors <- rownames(coefs2)
colnames(coefs2)[1] <- "Exponentiated Coefficients"
coefs2 <- coefs2[, c("Predictors", "Exponentiated Coefficients")]
coefs2 <- coefs2 |>
  filter(Predictors %in% c("(Intercept)", "age", "race", "bmi", "tumor_size", "tumor_grade", "lymph_nodes", 
                           "ER", "PR", "HER2", "Ki67", "surgery", "chemo", "hormonal_therapy"))
coefs2$`Exponentiated Coefficients`<- exp(coefs2$`Exponentiated Coefficients`)
rownames(coefs2) <- NULL
coefs2 |>
  kable()
Predictors Exponentiated Coefficients

# Create table with model performance results
tibble(
  "RMSE" = rmse_testB,
  "R^2" = r2_testB
) |> 
  kable()
RMSE R^2
0.6555808 0.1645233

Compare coefficients from datA & datB model

# Extract coefficients from both models
coefs_A <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(coef(enet.fit$finalModel, enet.fit$bestTune$lambda)))
coefs_B <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(coef(enet.test.b$finalModel, enet.test.b$bestTune$lambda)))

# Rename columns
colnames(coefs_A) <- "Coef_datA"
colnames(coefs_B) <- "Coef_datB"

# Add predictor names
coefs_A$Predictor <- rownames(coefs_A)
coefs_B$Predictor <- rownames(coefs_B)

# Merge both into one table
coef_comparison <- merge(coefs_A, coefs_B, by = "Predictor", all = TRUE)

# Add a column showing the difference
coef_comparison$Difference <- coef_comparison$Coef_datB - coef_comparison$Coef_datA

# Round for readability
coef_comparison$Coef_datA <- round(coef_comparison$Coef_datA, 4)
coef_comparison$Coef_datB <- round(coef_comparison$Coef_datB, 4)
coef_comparison$Difference <- round(coef_comparison$Difference, 4)

# Reorder columns
coef_comparison <- coef_comparison[, c("Predictor", "Coef_datA", "Coef_datB", "Difference")]

# Sort by absolute difference to highlight what changed most
coef_comparison <- coef_comparison[order(abs(coef_comparison$Difference), decreasing = TRUE), ]

rownames(coef_comparison) <- NULL

# Display table
coef_comparison |>
  kable(caption = "Comparison of Elastic Net Coefficients from datA and datB Models",
        col.names = c("Predictor", "datA Coefficient", "datB Coefficient", "Difference")) |> 
  add_footnote("Coefficients are on the log-PFS scale, Difference = datB minus datA. 
                Variables with zero coefficients were excluded by elastic net regularization.",
               notation = "none")
Comparison of Elastic Net Coefficients from datA and datB Models
Predictor datA Coefficient datB Coefficient Difference
(Intercept) 2.6597 2.9987 0.3390
HER21 -0.1732 0.0247 0.1979
PR1 0.0000 0.0963 0.0963
race4 -0.0245 0.0690 0.0934
tumor_grade2 0.0000 0.0748 0.0748
stage3 0.0000 -0.0741 -0.0741
race2 0.0000 -0.0401 -0.0401
lymph_nodes -0.0540 -0.0166 0.0374
race3 0.0109 -0.0244 -0.0353
chemo1 0.0000 -0.0309 -0.0309
ER1 0.1709 0.1449 -0.0260
surgery1 -0.0010 -0.0253 -0.0244
tumor_size -0.0545 -0.0422 0.0123
menopause1 0.0000 -0.0081 -0.0081
hormonal_therapy1 0.1590 0.1651 0.0061
stage2 0.0000 0.0031 0.0031
bmi 0.0000 -0.0015 -0.0015
tumor_grade3 -0.1057 -0.1066 -0.0008
age -0.0026 -0.0025 0.0001
Ki67 -0.0046 -0.0048 -0.0001

Note: Coefficients are on the log-PFS scale, Difference = datB minus datA. Variables with zero coefficients were excluded by elastic net regularization.
